title: marrubium nigrum
intro: Marrubium nigrum, also known as black horehound or black marjoram, is a perennial plant in the mint family native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's health benefits also include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate skin conditions like eczema and acne. Marrubium nigrum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used in herbal remedies like salves and poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
